judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with correct, well-cited git analysis; they are comparable, with Report 1 and Report 2 essentially equivalent in substance.

report_1.evidence: `main`. Per project documentation's dead-base-PR-trap rule ("Base every PR on the trunk")... Targeting `feat/weighted-scorer` as the base would be stacking on a branch whose purpose is already fulfilled; targeting `main` is the only stable option now.

report_2.evidence: `main`... `feat/weighted-scorer` is not a valid target — its work has already landed on `main` (see #2), so targeting it would create a PR against a branch that's effectively obsolete/superseded.

report_1.evidence: the branch was created from commit `ac9d505` ("branch: Created from HEAD"), which is the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer`... `git merge-base --is-ancestor ac9d505 main` returns NO... `main`'s current tip `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"... via a squash merge that produced a new commit hash (`1971f08`)... `origin` still carries the old `feat/weighted-scorer` ref pointing at `ac9d505`

report_2.evidence: It was branched from `feat/weighted-scorer` at commit `ac9d505`... merged into `main`, but via squash... `main`'s tip `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"... `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` fails: `feat/weighted-scorer` is not an ancestor of `main`... origin/feat/weighted-scorer, still present

report_1.evidence: Three-dot diff... shows 21 insertions / 3 deletions across 3 files: the entire `WEIGHTS`/`overlap`/weighted-`score()` rewrite of `scoring/scorer.py`... all of W1–W3 plus the real pairwise work... present only because the branch was forked from `feat/weighted-scorer` before that work was squash-merged into `main` as `1971f08`

report_2.evidence: a naive `main...feat/pairwise-metric` comparison... would show the changes from all five commits: W1, W2, W3 (weighted-scorer aggregation... rewritten `score()` in `scoring/scorer.py`... present in this branch purely as inherited history from the dependency branch it was built on top of before that dependency was squash-merged separately.

report_1.evidence: `git rebase --onto origin/main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` to explicitly drop the W1–W3 commits... after the `--onto` rebase, diff the branch against `main` again and confirm it's just the two real commits (`X1`, `X2`)... verify `scoring/scorer.py` shows no diff at all against `main`.

report_2.evidence: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` — this replays only the commit range... entirely dropping the redundant W1–W3 commits... Confirm the resulting diff against `main` is exactly the 2-commit, 5-line change (re-run `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` and `git log main..feat/pairwise-metric`)
